name: EMA Zone Inversion
author: @jtrader (concept), @masked_stat (open-source impl)
source: TradingView open-source scripts, X post Apr 2026

ml_filtered.filter: A/A+ setups only via ML grading model
ml_filtered.note: Works very well on trending markets. @masked_stat validated independently.

concept: Price creates a Fair Value Gap (3 consecutive same-direction candles), then inverts back through that gap while EMAs (33/50/200) remain stacked in trend direction. The zone bounce (wick into EMA 33/50 zone) between FVG and inversion confirms smart money defending the level.

indicators.EMA 50: Mid trend — zone between 33/50 is the key area
indicators.EMA 200: Slow trend — integrity check (all bars must close correct side)

long: Bearish FVG forms (3 bearish candles with gap)
Price holds above EMA 200 on every bar (integrity)
Zone bounce: wick touches EMA 33/50 zone
Inversion: price closes back above FVG top
short: Bullish FVG forms (3 bullish candles with gap)
Price holds below EMA 200 on every bar
Zone bounce: wick touches EMA 33/50 zone
Inversion: price closes below FVG bottom

exit_rules.stop_loss: Below pivot low (longs) or ATR-based. Configurable.

quality_filters.P1_single_pivot: Highest quality — only 1 pivot between FVG and inversion
quality_filters.P2_plus: Multiple pivots — lower quality, wider stop needed
quality_filters.zone_bounce: Wick must touch EMA 33/50 zone (smart money defense)
quality_filters.ema200_integrity: Every bar between FVG and inversion closes correct side of 200 EMA

risk_rules: FVG max age 30 bars — stale gaps lose meaning
One FVG consumed per inversion (no reuse)
Best on liquid instruments during active sessions

assessment.verdict: Legitimate edge, thin at PF 1.06 baseline. Quality filters (P:1, zone bounce) likely improve it significantly. Worth forward testing alongside BB+RSI.
assessment.potential: High — with P:1 filter and session timing, could reach PF 1.3+

_recommendations: BTC 1H: Very few trades — try longer timeframe or wider date range for more data
